data.title: As a developer implementing sg-based kudos triggers, I can trust sg --json=stream NDJSON output before any sg-dependent trigger is built
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Spike S0 — validate sg (ast-grep) --json=stream behavior: exit codes, NDJSON schema, and absence-detection method.
**Why**: Stories B, E depend on sg --json=stream producing reliable, parseable output. Building those stories on unconfirmed behavior risks implementation failures.
**Scope**:
- IN: Live validation of sg exit codes (0=no matches, 1=matches found, other=error), NDJSON schema fields (path, range, text, rule_id), and command -v sg absence-detection
- OUT: Any implementation code; diff-to-sg-scope.sh itself (Story B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sg exit codes are confirmed via live command (0=no matches, 1=matches found)
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-11 (Spike S0 validates sg exit code behavior)
- When this story is complete, NDJSON schema fields from sg --json=stream are documented with sample output
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-11
- When this story is complete, command -v sg is confirmed as the correct absence-detection method (not exit code)
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-11
- When this story is complete, findings are recorded as a ticket comment: exit code 0=<result>, 1=<result>, NDJSON schema: <field list>, absence detection: confirmed
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-11
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for any spike harness scripts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
